tr.td: Maximize kthread uptime during long runs

div.p: SQPOLL trades deterministic resource allocation (1 dedicated CPU core) for the total elimination of syscall overhead. The architectural break-even point is reached when the cumulative cost of syscall context switches and overhead exceeds the capacity of the dedicated polling core—typically found in workloads exceeding 100,000 IOPS.

g.text: ━━━ Non-SQPOLL: CPU scales with submission rate
